Title: Verify that POST /carts returns 201 Created and cartId when creating a new cart, and GET /carts/{cartId} returns 200 OK and empty items list
Pre-conditions: None.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send an empty POST request to /carts.
2. Verify response status code is 201 and retrieve the cartId.
3. Send a GET request to /carts/{cartId} using the created cartId.
4. Verify status code is 200.
5. Check that the created timestamp is present and the items list is empty.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status for creation: 201 Created
• Response body contains created=true and a non-null cartId.
• HTTP Status for retrieval: 200 OK
• Response body contains cart details with an empty items array.

Title: Verify that GET /carts/{cartId}/items returns 200 OK and an empty items list when a new empty cart is requested
Pre-conditions: A new empty cart is created.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Create a new empty cart and retrieve its cartId.
2. Send a GET request to /carts/{cartId}/items.
3. Verify status code is 200 and the response body returns an empty array.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 200 OK
• Response body contains an empty JSON array of items [].

Title: Verify that POST /carts/{cartId}/items returns 201 Created and successfully adds an item when a valid productId and quantity are provided
Pre-conditions: A valid empty cart exists, and a valid available product exists.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Create a new empty cart and retrieve cartId.
2. Retrieve a random available product and determine a random quantity less than or equal to stock.
3. Send a POST request to /carts/{cartId}/items with productId and quantity.
4. Verify status code is 201 and retrieve the itemId from the response.
5. Send a GET request to /carts/{cartId}/items and verify the cart contains exactly 1 item matching the added productId, quantity, and itemId.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 201 Created
• Response body contains created=true and a non-null itemId.
• Retrieval of cart items verifies the product was added with the specified quantity and itemId.

Title: Verify that POST /carts/{cartId}/items returns 201 Created and adds the item when quantity is exactly equal to the product's current stock
Pre-conditions: A valid empty cart exists, and a valid available product exists.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Create a new empty cart and retrieve cartId.
2. Retrieve a random available product and set quantity to its exact currentStock.
3. Send a POST request to /carts/{cartId}/items with productId and quantity equal to stock.
4. Verify status code is 201 and retrieve itemId.
5. Retrieve cart items and verify the product is added with the exact stock quantity.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 201 Created
• The item is successfully added and matches the exact stock quantity when retrieved.

Title: Verify that POST /carts/{cartId}/items returns 201 Created for multiple unique products added sequentially to the same cart
Pre-conditions: A valid empty cart exists.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Create a new empty cart and retrieve cartId.
2. Retrieve 10 random unique available products and random valid quantities.
3. Sequentially send POST requests to /carts/{cartId}/items for each of the 10 items.
4. Verify status code is 201 for each addition.
5. Send a GET request to /carts/{cartId}/items and verify it contains exactly 10 items with correct productId and quantity pairings.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 201 Created for all 10 requests.
• Retrieval of cart items returns an array of size 10 containing all added products with their correct quantities.

Title: Verify that POST /carts/{cartId}/items returns 400 Bad Request and validation error when attempting to add a product that has already been added to the cart
Pre-conditions: A valid cart exists containing a specific product.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Create a cart and add an available product with a valid quantity.
2. Attempt to add the exact same product again to the same cart.
3. Retrieve the response.
4. Verify the HTTP status code is 400.
5. Verify the error response contains "This product has already been added to cart".
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 400 Bad Request
• Response body contains an error object: {"error": "This product has already been added to cart"}

Title: Verify that POST /carts/{cartId}/items returns 400 Bad Request and validation error when attempting to add an out-of-stock product
Pre-conditions: An empty cart exists, and an out-of-stock (inStock=false) product exists in the inventory.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Create a cart.
2. Retrieve a random non-available product.
3. Send a POST request to add this product with quantity = 1.
4. Retrieve the response.
5. Verify the HTTP status code is 400.
6. Verify the error response contains "This product is not in stock and cannot be ordered".
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 400 Bad Request
• Response body contains an error object: {"error": "This product is not in stock and cannot be ordered"}

Title: Verify that POST /carts/{cartId}/items returns 400 Bad Request and validation error when the requested quantity exceeds the product's current stock

3. Send a POST request to add the product with quantity set to currentStock + 1.
4. Retrieve the response.

6. Verify the error response contains "The quantity requested exceeds the current stock".
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 400 Bad Request
• Response body contains an error object: {"error": "The quantity requested exceeds the current stock"}

Title: Verify that DELETE /carts/{cartId}/items/{itemId} returns 204 No Content and successfully deletes the item from the cart

3. Send a DELETE request to /carts/{cartId}/items/{itemId}.
4. Verify the status code is 204.
5. Send a GET request to /carts/{cartId}/items and verify the items list is empty.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 204 No Content
• Retrieval of cart items verifies that the item is no longer in the cart.

Title: Verify that DELETE /carts/{cartId}/items/{itemId} returns 204 No Content for sequential deletions of multiple items in the same cart
Pre-conditions: A cart exists containing two unique items.

2. Add two unique available products to the cart, obtaining their respective itemId1 and itemId2.
3. Send a DELETE request for itemId1 and verify status code is 204.
4. Send a DELETE request for itemId2 and verify status code is 204.
5. Send a GET request to /carts/{cartId}/items and verify that the items list is empty.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 204 No Content for both requests.
• Retrieval of cart items verifies that both items have been removed.

Title: Verify that DELETE /carts/{cartId}/items/{itemId} returns 404 Not Found and validation error when attempting to delete the same item a second time

Test Execution Steps:

2. Add an available product to the cart and retrieve its itemId.
3. Delete the item and verify status code is 204.
4. Attempt to delete the same item again.
5. Retrieve the response and verify the HTTP status code is 404.
6. Verify the error response contains "No item with id".
Expected Result:
• First delete returns 204 No Content.
• Second delete returns 404 Not Found with an error object containing the message "No item with id".

Title: Verify that PATCH /carts/{cartId}/items/{itemId} returns 204 No Content and successfully updates the quantity of an item in the cart
Pre-conditions: A cart exists containing one item. The product has a stock of at least 2.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Create a cart.
2. Retrieve a product with stock >= 2 and add it to the cart with quantity = 1, obtaining the itemId.
3. Send a PATCH request to /carts/{cartId}/items/{itemId} with a request body specifying a new quantity of 2.
4. Verify the status code is 204.
5. Send a GET request to /carts/{cartId}/items and verify the quantity of the item is updated to 2.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 204 No Content
• Retrieval of cart items verifies that the item quantity is updated to 2.
